title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
problem_statement: Return the level order traversal of a binary tree’s node values.

examples.explanation: Nodes are grouped by distance from the root.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [0, 2000]
intuition: Use a queue to traverse the tree breadth-first and collect nodes layer by layer.

approaches.name: Breadth-first search
approaches.description: Use a queue and process one level at a time, collecting values for each level before moving on.
approaches.why_not: Recursive DFS does not naturally group nodes by level without extra bookkeeping.

interview_qa.q: What data structure supports level order traversal?
interview_qa.a: A queue supports breadth-first traversal across tree levels.

interview_qa.q: How do you know when a level ends?
interview_qa.a: By processing a fixed number of nodes equal to the current queue size.
